The note given is F♯. A diminished sixth below F♯ is D♯♯ (double-sharp D), which is enharmonically equivalent to E.  
Among the options, the correct root written as a double-sharp A (A𝄪) would be a diminished sixth below F♯, but the options show A, B, C, and double-sharp A.  
The correct answer is D, as it is the only option with a double-sharp, matching the required diminished sixth interval.  

\boxed{D}
